perturbation.original_text: On the commencement of the Term Distributor will issue to the Company an irrevocable letter of credit ("LC") in the amount of Five Hundred Thousand Dollars ($500,000), the form of which is attached hereto as Exhibit A and incorporated herein by reference. The LC shall have a two (2) month term, and shall be renewed for five (5) consecutive bimonthly periods. A minimum of a $250,000.00 purchase order must be received by Company by the first of each month for a total (12) month period. The Company may draw funds from the LC to pay for Distributor's purchases, which are not paid according to the terms in Section 2.7. Prices for the EnergySaver units are Page -2- provided by the Company as Exhibit C. The Company will be entitled to draw against the LC pursuant to the terms of the LC.
perturbation.changed_text: On the commencement of the Term Distributor will issue to the Company a letter of credit ("LC"), the form of which is attached hereto as Exhibit A and incorporated herein by reference. Prices for the EnergySaver units are Page -2- provided by the Company as Exhibit C. The Company will be entitled to draw against the LC pursuant to the terms of the LC.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific details about the LC's amount, term, renewal frequency, and minimum purchase order requirements, this creates an in-text contradiction because the reference to drawing against the LC "pursuant to the terms of the LC" lacks substance. There are no specific terms of the LC to refer to within section 1.6. The reference to section 2.7 also contributes to the confusion, because this section will refer back to the letter of credit as a viable method for payment, when the exact requirements of the LC have been removed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distributor shall pay Company within thirty (30) days of Distributor's or, as the case may be, the end-user's receipt of Products.
perturbation.justification: The omission of key terms related to the letter of credit (LC) such as its amount, term, renewal frequency, and minimum purchase order requirements directly contradicts the implied assumption in the contradicted text that the LC is a viable payment method with clearly defined parameters. The original text established the LC, the changed text removes key elements of the original terms, while the contradicted text operates under the original assumption, resulting in an unresolvable in-text contradiction. This adheres to the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3.3 Warranty. Company shall at all times make reasonable efforts to maintain quality control and to deliver Products to Distributor which, when received by Distributor, or, as the case may be, the end-user, are properly and adequately packaged and contained, fully assembled (except for miscellaneous components which may be shipped separately to prevent damage in transit), fully functional and otherwise in conformance with the warranties set forth herein. Company warrants that the Products will be designed, manufactured, constructed, assembled and packaged in a workmanlike manner and that such Products shall be fully functional and fit for their intended purposes. Company further warrants that the Products sold hereunder shall be free from defects in design, materials and workmanship for a period of twenty-four (24) months after delivery to Distributor's end-user. The Company shall not be liable for defective Products, except as provided in this Agreement. The Distributor at all times shall comply with all requirements of the Magnuson-Moss Warranty-Federal Trade Commission Improvement Act and similar federal and state laws and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.3 Warranty. Company shall at all times make reasonable efforts to maintain quality control and to deliver Products to Distributor which, when received by Distributor, or, as the case may be, the end-user, are properly and adequately packaged and contained, fully assembled (except for miscellaneous components which may be shipped separately to prevent damage in transit), fully functional and otherwise in conformance with the warranties set forth herein. The Company shall not be liable for defective Products, except as provided in this Agreement. The Distributor at all times shall comply with all requirements of the Magnuson-Moss Warranty-Federal Trade Commission Improvement Act and similar federal and state laws and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific warranty details (workmanlike manner, fitness for purpose, freedom from defects for 24 months), the introductory sentence references "warranties set forth herein," but those warranties no longer exist. Section 3.4 refers back to those warranties which were removed, to create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If, within the twenty-four (24) month warranty period set forth above, Company received from Distributor or any of Distributor's end-user's a notice which may be oral notice confirmed in writing) that any of the Products sold hereunder do not meet the Warranties specified above, Company shall thereupon correct each such defect by providing the necessary repairs, and/or replacement parts, or if necessary, Products. Company shall promptly respond to any timely notice of defect. Unless otherwise expressly agreed to in writing by Distributor or Distributor's and-user, Company shall bear the reasonable expense of all labor, materials and shipping expended or used in connection with the correction of any defects in the Products occasioned by the non-conformance of the Product with Company's warranty as set forth herein. Company shall be entitled to dispute whether a Product is defective. In the event that Company is unable or unwilling to promptly perform any warranty work without reasonable cause and following full and fair opportunity to do so, or in the event of the necessity for emergency repairs of a defective Product for which there is no reasonable possibility of performance by Company, Distributor may perform such warranty work or hire a third party to perform such warranty work and the reasonable cost thereof shall be paid by Company.
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the explicit warranty details (workmanlike manner, fitness for purpose, freedom from defects for 24 months) that were present in the original text's Section 3.3. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text in Section 3.4 refers back to the 'twenty-four (24) month warranty period set forth above' and the 'Warranties specified above,' which no longer exist in the modified version of Section 3.3, rendering Section 3.4 unenforceable as it references non-existent warranty terms.
